text: ♪ “Ready Steady Go” by Harry Styles ♪

speaker: Tim
text: Good morning.

speaker: Tim
text: Thank you for joining us today at Apple Park

speaker: Tim
text: and welcome to WWDC.

speaker: Tim
text: We all look forward to this event and this week

speaker: Tim
text: as we get to share our latest platform advancements and innovations

speaker: Tim
text: with our developer community and with our users.

speaker: Tim
text: WWDC also gives us the chance

speaker: Tim
text: to celebrate our incredible global community of developers.

speaker: Tim
text: The energy around Apple platforms has never been stronger.

speaker: Tim
text: Developers are building more apps than ever

speaker: Tim
text: with well over 1,000 submissions to the App Store every hour.

speaker: Tim
text: We’re also investing in the next generation

speaker: Tim
text: through 20 Apple Developer Academies around the world

speaker: Tim
text: which have already helped tens of thousands of students

speaker: Tim
text: begin their journeys.

speaker: Tim
text: And with WWDC, we hope to build on that momentum, inspiring even

speaker: Tim
text: more incredible app experiences for our users.

speaker: Tim
text: At Apple, we've always believed

speaker: Tim
text: that technology should be personal, powerful, and easy to use.

speaker: Tim
text: The tight integration of our hardware and our software

speaker: Tim
text: unlocks the full potential of each of our products.

speaker: Tim
text: That same philosophy drives us

speaker: Tim
text: to design our operating systems to work together seamlessly

speaker: Tim
text: and to create a common foundation of technologies

speaker: Tim
text: to help developers build great experiences across their apps.

speaker: Tim
text: We’ve seen that when we put powerful tools into people’s hands,

speaker: Tim
text: they can do extraordinary things from extraordinary places.

speaker: Tim
text: Places we only could have dreamed of.

speaker: Tim
text: This is what WWDC is all about, introducing new technologies

speaker: Tim
text: and innovations that push the limits of what's possible.

speaker: Tim
text: Today we're looking forward to sharing some exciting announcements

speaker: Tim
text: including our latest advancements in Apple Intelligence and Siri.

speaker: Tim
text: With that, I'd like to hand it off to Craig to get us started.

speaker: Tim
text: ♪  “Clouds Will Carry Me To Sleep” by Gelli Haha  ♪

speaker: Craig
text: Let’s talk about our next set of releases

speaker: Craig
text: where we have some exciting updates to share.

speaker: Craig
text: This year, we put our focus into three areas.

speaker: Craig
text: First, improvements that make our platforms more responsive

speaker: Craig
text: and easier to use in your day-to-day life.

speaker: Craig
text: Second, updates in trust and safety,

speaker: Craig
text: where we’re building on our commitment

speaker: Craig
text: to provide a safe and trusted platform for kids.

speaker: Craig
text: And third, a big leap forward for Apple Intelligence,

speaker: Craig
text: with an innovative architecture that unlocks a new Siri across platforms.

speaker: Craig
text: Along with many helpful features

speaker: Craig
text: integrated into the apps you use every day.

speaker: Craig
text: This work applies to our new releases across all of our platforms,

speaker: Craig
text: from iOS and iPadOS,

speaker: Craig
text: to watchOS and tvOS, visionOS and macOS.

speaker: Craig
text: Speaking of macOS,

speaker: Craig
text: for those of you that are WWDC regulars,

speaker: Craig
text: you of course know that this is the moment

speaker: Craig
text: where I relay the latest exploits of Apple’s crack marketing team,

speaker: Craig
text: and their, shall we say, unconventional methods

speaker: Craig
text: for naming macOS releases.

speaker: Craig
text: In this case, I'm afraid the story is incomplete.

speaker: Craig
text: Last I saw them,

speaker: Craig
text: they just spilled out of their recently installed

speaker: Craig
text: Apple Park “experiential ideation yurt”

speaker: Craig
text: and piled into their microbus.

speaker: Craig
text: I tried to catch them, but they just handed me

speaker: Craig
text: this note out the window before motoring northward.

speaker: Craig
text: So here's all I've got.

speaker: Craig
text: ♪  “Beat of the Drum” by Homestead  Wolfe ♪

speaker: Craig
text: “Dude, our chakra alignment

speaker: Craig
text: has set our compass toward the summer of love.

speaker: Craig
text: But like, further.

speaker: Craig
text: Our corporeal forms know no earthly tether.

speaker: Craig
text: We shall float on a span of gold

speaker: Craig
text: over infinite seas, flying so high.

speaker: Craig
text: Marketing is such a great job.”

speaker: Craig
text: Okay, well, I’m afraid they haven’t returned

speaker: Craig
text: and I’m lacking the sensory amplifiers to crack this code.

speaker: Craig
text: So I guess the great era of macOS names must come to an end.

speaker: Craig
text: It's Golden Gate, man.

speaker: Craig
text: ♪ “Beat of the Drum” by Homestead  Wolfe ♪

speaker: Craig
text: [ Laughter ]

speaker: Craig
text: Oh thank goodness. Perfect.

speaker: Craig
text: Our next version of macOS is macOS Golden Gate.

speaker: Craig
text: Alright, with that behind us, let’s start with improvements

speaker: Craig
text: that will make our platforms better than ever.

speaker: Craig
text: Our products are an integral part of daily life

speaker: Craig
text: helping us at work, at school, at home, and everywhere in between.

speaker: Craig
text: So naturally we all have high expectations for them.

speaker: Craig
text: And we’re always challenging ourselves

speaker: Craig
text: to make our products ever more responsive, even more reliable,

speaker: Craig
text: and that much more delightful to use.

speaker: Craig
text: So instead of just introducing a host of new features,

speaker: Craig
text: we’re also taking the features you already rely on

speaker: Craig
text: and making them even better.

speaker: Craig
text: Because we believe the best operating systems

speaker: Craig
text: aren’t just built on big breakthroughs.

speaker: Craig
text: They're built on sweating the details.

speaker: Craig
text: To tell you more, here’s Stacey.

speaker: Craig
text: ♪ “Of Course” by Yebba  ♪

speaker: Stacey
text: Attention to detail has always been core to Apple’s DNA.

speaker: Stacey
text: And this year, our teams doubled down on our relentless dedication

speaker: Stacey
text: to make the experience feel more polished and intuitive.

speaker: Stacey
text: We scoured every part of the OS for opportunities to refine our systems

speaker: Stacey
text: from the UI to the foundations.

speaker: Stacey
text: Nothing was off limits, no enhancement too small.

speaker: Stacey
text: We made things faster, smoother, even easier to use

speaker: Stacey
text: and we took care of a bunch of things you’ve been asking about.

speaker: Stacey
text: We’re excited to share some highlights of this work with you.

speaker: Stacey
text: To kick things off, let’s talk about

speaker: Stacey
text: one of the key themes of this effort: design.

speaker: Stacey
text: To tell you more, here’s Shubham.

speaker: Shubham
text: Last year,

speaker: Shubham
text: we introduced our most ambitious cross-platform design update ever

speaker: Shubham
text: with Liquid Glass,

speaker: Shubham
text: which made apps and experiences even more expressive and delightful.

speaker: Shubham
text: Like with all major design updates,

speaker: Shubham
text: there’s a natural process where we take a bold leap forward

speaker: Shubham
text: and then we continue to iterate.

speaker: Shubham
text: Part of how we do this is by listening to users and developers.

speaker: Shubham
text: Our team really appreciates your feedback and we considered it deeply

speaker: Shubham
text: as we refined the new design over the past year.

speaker: Shubham
text: Now, we're making some additional refinements

speaker: Shubham
text: starting with updating the foundations of how Liquid Glass is built.

speaker: Shubham
text: To ensure exceptional readability, we tuned Liquid Glass

speaker: Shubham
text: so it diffuses complex content behind it much more effectively,

speaker: Shubham
text: while also creating more depth and separation.

speaker: Shubham
text: While we think this is a great new default look,

speaker: Shubham
text: we also know that some users

speaker: Shubham
text: would like Liquid Glass to be even more clear,

speaker: Shubham
text: and others prefer a more tinted appearance.

speaker: Shubham
text: Since everyone’s preference varies,

speaker: Shubham
text: we’re adding a new slider and settings to adjust Liquid Glass,

speaker: Shubham
text: so you can set it anywhere from ultra clear to fully tinted.

speaker: Shubham
text: It's a whole new way to personalize your experience across apps.

speaker: Shubham
text: And for developers who have already adopted Liquid Glass,

speaker: Shubham
text: these customizations apply in your apps right away.

speaker: Shubham
text: In addition to improving Liquid Glass, we also wanted to reincorporate

speaker: Shubham
text: some of the cornerstones of macOS design

speaker: Shubham
text: that our users have always loved.

speaker: Shubham
text: For example, to help better distinguish larger groups of controls

speaker: Shubham
text: and text from the content beneath them,

speaker: Shubham
text: there is now a more uniform toolbar across the top of apps.

speaker: Shubham
text: This keeps text labels and headings legible

speaker: Shubham
text: while also providing greater structure.

speaker: Shubham
text: And to further refine the structure of apps on Mac,

speaker: Shubham
text: sidebars now expand to the very edges of the window.

speaker: Shubham
text: This helps reduce distractions along the left edge.

speaker: Shubham
text: And it delivers the same beautiful refractions as content scrolls underneath

speaker: Shubham
text: to create a more expansive feeling across your apps.

speaker: Shubham
text: Sidebar icons also regain their color, making it easier to distinguish apps

speaker: Shubham
text: and identify which window is active in the foreground.

speaker: Shubham
text: And every window on macOS now has the same tighter corner radius

speaker: Shubham
text: ensuring greater consistency across all of your apps,

speaker: Shubham
text: even if they haven’t been updated.

speaker: Shubham
text: Finally, let's talk about app icons.

speaker: Shubham
text: Last year, we redesigned all of our app icons to establish

speaker: Shubham
text: a more harmonious look across apps and platforms.

speaker: Shubham
text: This year, we’re taking this new design even further

speaker: Shubham
text: by integrating additional layers of Liquid Glass

speaker: Shubham
text: directly into the icon artwork itself.

speaker: Shubham
text: Not only does the refraction between these layers add character to each icon,

speaker: Shubham
text: but this new approach also makes icons appear sharper and more defined.

speaker: Shubham
text: They look great in your Dock, on your Home Screen,

speaker: Shubham
text: or when they're putting to an all-clear look.

speaker: Shubham
text: Together these updates culminate in an even more focused

speaker: Shubham
text: and approachable experience across your apps and across platforms.

speaker: Shubham
text: Now back to Stacey.

speaker: Stacey
text: These design improvements are just the beginning.

speaker: Stacey
text: As we embarked on this journey

speaker: Stacey
text: to raise the bar for our products even higher,

speaker: Stacey
text: we also spent a lot of time digging into the fundamentals.

speaker: Stacey
text: Things like memory usage,

speaker: Stacey
text: CPU utilization, networking operations, display rendering, I could go on and on.

speaker: Stacey
text: But really the point is this:

speaker: Stacey
text: we optimized the parts of the system that make a big difference

speaker: Stacey
text: in the responsiveness of our products.

speaker: Stacey
text: For example, system animations are smoother,

speaker: Stacey
text: like swiping between Home Screen pages on your iPhone,

speaker: Stacey
text: entering Mission Control, or moving between spaces on Mac.

speaker: Stacey
text: Plus, a lot of things are faster.

speaker: Stacey
text: I'll just name a few.

speaker: Stacey
text: First, iPhone and iPad apps launch up to 30% faster.

speaker: Stacey
text: It’s kind of unbelievable that it could get even faster,

speaker: Stacey
text: but the team found a way to preload the key data

speaker: Stacey
text: apps need when you open them, so they’re ready to go.

speaker: Stacey
text: This applies to third-party apps too.

speaker: Stacey
text: And content across the system loads faster.

speaker: Stacey
text: For instance, after you shot some new photos,

speaker: Stacey
text: it used to take a beat for them to load in your library.

speaker: Stacey
text: Now, new photos appear up to 70% faster.

speaker: Stacey
text: And when you share those photos or other files

speaker: Stacey
text: with a friend using AirDrop, they’ll transfer up to 80% faster.

speaker: Stacey
text: And browsing files and transferring them from iPad to an external drive

speaker: Stacey
text: is up to five times faster,

speaker: Stacey
text: making it just as fast as the Finder on Mac.

speaker: Stacey
text: We're also doing something that will make older iPhones

speaker: Stacey
text: feel even more responsive, which has to do with our CPU scheduler.

speaker: Stacey
text: The CPU scheduler is a key system component

speaker: Stacey
text: that manages CPU resources across workloads

speaker: Stacey
text: as you use your iPhone throughout your day.

speaker: Stacey
text: Even when you’re doing a ton of things at once,

speaker: Stacey
text: requiring a whole lot of compute.

speaker: Stacey
text: The CPU scheduler ensures the right work is executed at precisely the right time.

speaker: Stacey
text: On our newer iPhones, iOS already has an advanced CPU scheduler.

speaker: Stacey
text: This year we further optimized it

speaker: Stacey
text: so it's even more efficient when handling performance-intensive workloads.

speaker: Stacey
text: And not only that, but we also figured out a way to bring it to older models,

speaker: Stacey
text: all the way back to iPhone 11.

speaker: Stacey
text: And yes, that does mean that iOS 27 is supported on iPhone 11

speaker: Stacey
text: and all of the same iPhone models as iOS 26.

speaker: Stacey
text: This continues our industry-leading support

speaker: Stacey
text: and means iOS 27 is available to more users than any iOS release ever.

speaker: Stacey
text: There’s also some work we’ve done

speaker: Stacey
text: that’s about getting out of your way, clearing the path so you can work,

speaker: Stacey
text: communicate, and find the things you’re looking for without speed bumps.

speaker: Stacey
text: Like with network transitions, we're making it more seamless

speaker: Stacey
text: for your iPhone to transition between cellular and Wi-Fi networks.

speaker: Stacey
text: We all know those moments where you find yourself jumping

speaker: Stacey
text: into Control Center to manually toggle off Wi-Fi

speaker: Stacey
text: to force a cellular connection.

speaker: Stacey
text: Like when you walk past your favorite coffee shop

speaker: Stacey
text: with that extra strong Wi-Fi signal.

speaker: Stacey
text: Or try to go online as you get off a flight

speaker: Stacey
text: and realize you’re still connected to the airline’s network.

speaker: Stacey
text: To help you avoid moments like these, we’ve made your iPhone

speaker: Stacey
text: smarter about when to stay connected and when to switch.

speaker: Stacey
text: And for those times when you’re in a low bandwidth situation,

speaker: Stacey
text: sending a big photo or video in Messages will no longer slow your conversation.

speaker: Stacey
text: You’ll see a new send indicator for each message

speaker: Stacey
text: when things are taking a little longer to go through,

speaker: Stacey
text: giving you clarity on exactly what has and hasn't yet been delivered.

speaker: Stacey
text: There's one last area I want to mention where we've made a big push

speaker: Stacey
text: to help you accomplish what you need with greater ease and reliability.

speaker: Stacey
text: And that’s Search.

speaker: Stacey
text: We’ve all had that moment where you search for something

speaker: Stacey
text: you know is there, but it just won’t show up.

speaker: Stacey
text: So on iOS, iPadOS, and macOS, we’ve rebuilt the foundation of search

speaker: Stacey
text: that powers Spotlight, Photos, and Mail.

speaker: Stacey
text: At its core is the search index,

speaker: Stacey
text: a rich catalog your device builds of all your content

speaker: Stacey
text: so it can understand what you have and where to find it.

speaker: Stacey
text: We re-architected the index so it’s more stable, more efficient,

speaker: Stacey
text: and more comprehensive of content both old and new.

speaker: Stacey
text: After you update, our new search infrastructure

speaker: Stacey
text: goes to work indexing the content of your device

speaker: Stacey
text: so it has a complete picture of what's already there.

speaker: Stacey
text: And as new content comes in, it's indexed almost immediately.

speaker: Stacey
text: So when you go to search, you'll find what you're looking for

speaker: Stacey
text: Plus, in Mail, a completely new ranking system

speaker: Stacey
text: surfaces even more relevant results in your Top Hits.

speaker: Stacey
text: So the email you're looking for is more likely

speaker: Stacey
text: to be the very first thing you see, even if it was sent months ago.

speaker: Stacey
text: Those are just some highlights of the work we’ve done this year

speaker: Stacey
text: to make our products smoother and more responsive.

speaker: Stacey
text: There are also some great features across your apps and products

speaker: Stacey
text: that we’re excited to deliver, including some we’ve been hearing about from you!

speaker: Stacey
text: Let me give you a handful of examples.

speaker: Stacey
text: In Photos, your iCloud shared albums can now include

speaker: Stacey
text: all of the photos and videos from your latest adventures,

speaker: Stacey
text: because your friends on Android or Windows

speaker: Stacey
text: can join and contribute their own captures to the album.

speaker: Stacey
text: And what’s more, Shared Albums now supports full-resolution sharing.

speaker: Stacey
text: In the Health app, Cycle Tracking now has support for perimenopause and menopause.

speaker: Stacey
text: You can be notified when your cycle patterns are suggestive of perimenopause.

speaker: Stacey
text: Log your related symptoms and read educational information

speaker: Stacey
text: so you can understand more about what's going on with your body

speaker: Stacey
text: and be better prepared to talk to your doctor.

speaker: Stacey
text: On AirPods, we’re delivering custom EQ

speaker: Stacey
text: so you can further personalize how your AirPods sound.

speaker: Stacey
text: ♪ “Written Into Changes” by Avalon Emerson ♪

speaker: Stacey
text: On Apple Vision Pro,

speaker: Stacey
text: you can now turn panoramas you’ve shot into spatial scenes

speaker: Stacey
text: with incredible depth and realism, making your memories even more immersive.

speaker: Stacey
text: And you can use those panoramas as your environment,

speaker: Stacey
text: surrounding yourself with a moment that is personal to you.

speaker: Stacey
text: And lastly, over in Maps, we're giving Flyover an incredible boost.

speaker: Stacey
text: Using aerial imagery combined with vision intelligence models,

speaker: Stacey
text: everything is rendered in stunning, sharp detail.

speaker: Stacey
text: From beautiful architectural details to the shapes of individual trees,

speaker: Stacey
text: to the way light reflects off the glass of skyscrapers,

speaker: Stacey
text: you’ll see cities around the world like never before.

speaker: Stacey
text: We are thrilled about how these improvements

speaker: Stacey
text: touch so many parts of the experience across our platforms.

speaker: Stacey
text: Now, back to Craig.

speaker: Craig
text: With meaningful design improvements,

speaker: Craig
text: enhancements that make things even more responsive,

speaker: Craig
text: and some great new features,

speaker: Craig
text: we think the experience you have with your Apple products

speaker: Craig
text: will be more delightful than ever.

speaker: Craig
text: Now let’s talk about Trust and safety.

speaker: Craig
text: We build products that empower people and enrich their lives,

speaker: Craig
text: while helping them stay safe.

speaker: Craig
text: We provide end-to-end encryption to help protect your conversations,

speaker: Craig
text: strong privacy protections in Safari,

speaker: Craig
text: app permissions that keep you in control of your location, photos, and more,

speaker: Craig
text: and life-saving features like Crash Detection.

speaker: Craig
text: Our long-standing commitment to safety is especially important

speaker: Craig
text: when it comes to kids and teens

speaker: Craig
text: who rely on our devices to stay in touch,

speaker: Craig
text: to explore creativity and enhance learning,

speaker: Craig
text: and to build their independence as they grow.

speaker: Craig
text: And as parents ourselves,

speaker: Craig
text: we’re committed to building a safe and trusted platform for kids.

speaker: Craig
text: So this year we’re taking a big step, expanding our child safety features

speaker: Craig
text: with powerful and intuitive tools.

speaker: Craig
text: To tell you about our approach, here's Sumbul.

speaker: Sumbul
text: Our work in this area is grounded in two core principles.

speaker: Sumbul
text: First, we recognize that every child is unique,

speaker: Sumbul
text: and parents are in the best position to decide what works for their family.

speaker: Sumbul
text: Second, we believe in shaping Apple’s child safety features

speaker: Sumbul
text: based on expert health research.

speaker: Sumbul
text: The best available research

speaker: Sumbul
text: from clinical and child development experts,

speaker: Sumbul
text: as well as online safety experts, emphasizes the importance

speaker: Sumbul
text: of balancing learning, creativity, and connection

speaker: Sumbul
text: with establishing boundaries around technology use.

speaker: Sumbul
text: This is critical in protecting adequate time for in-person social interaction,

speaker: Sumbul
text: academics, physical activity, and sleep,

speaker: Sumbul
text: all of which are essential for healthy child development.

speaker: Sumbul
text: The research also shows that kids under 18 using personal devices

speaker: Sumbul
text: benefit from age-based protections guided by adult supervision.

speaker: Sumbul
text: And for those under the age of 13,

speaker: Sumbul
text: access to personal devices should be limited

speaker: Sumbul
text: and expanded when a child is ready.

speaker: Sumbul
text: As parents, we want our kids to develop healthy digital habits.

speaker: Sumbul
text: To help parents accomplish this,

speaker: Sumbul
text: the American Academy of Pediatrics created the Family Media Plan.

speaker: Sumbul
text: And we’re working with them to adapt it into a guide for parents

speaker: Sumbul
text: that helps them establish those healthy digital habits

speaker: Sumbul
text: using our child safety features.

speaker: Sumbul
text: We also continue to collaborate with researchers

speaker: Sumbul
text: to understand the impact of technology on children’s well-being

speaker: Sumbul
text: and are committed to advancing the science in this area.

speaker: Sumbul
text: We know many families are looking for help

speaker: Sumbul
text: to make sure their kids experience only what parents think they're ready for.

speaker: Sumbul
text: Here’s Raja to show how they can get started.

speaker: Raja
text: As parents, we want our kids to get started on the right foot.

speaker: Raja
text: So it’s important to know that the first

speaker: Raja
text: and most important step is to create a Child Account.

speaker: Raja
text: It immediately enables safeguards across the system, tailored to their age,

speaker: Raja
text: like blocking adult websites, only allowing age-appropriate media,

speaker: Raja
text: and setting age-based restrictions in the App Store.

speaker: Raja
text: A Child Account also provides access to powerful parental controls.

speaker: Raja
text: If your kids aren’t on a Child Account today

speaker: Raja
text: don’t worry, you can convert their existing account.

speaker: Raja
text: Building off the child account, this year we focused

speaker: Raja
text: on tackling the things parents are most concerned about,

speaker: Raja
text: like what content our kids can see, who they’re allowed to talk to,

speaker: Raja
text: when kids have access, and how parents can guide their kids' digital journey.

speaker: Raja
text: And we’re providing parents with recommendations

speaker: Raja
text: based on expert guidance to help them decide

speaker: Raja
text: what’s right for their kids.

speaker: Raja
text: Let’s start with what content kids can see.

speaker: Raja
text: On one hand, there's so many benefits to your child having their own device.

speaker: Raja
text: You rest easier since you can stay in touch, know where they are,

speaker: Raja
text: and provide them with great apps that help them learn and grow.

speaker: Raja
text: But we also worry about opening the door to too much too quickly,

speaker: Raja
text: and letting kids access things they're not ready for yet.

speaker: Raja
text: Starting this year, parents can ensure their kids start focused

speaker: Raja
text: by giving them access to only the content they're comfortable with.

speaker: Raja
text: Then, gradually and when appropriate, add more over time.

speaker: Raja
text: It begins with an easy-to-use Setup Assistant,

speaker: Raja
text: where parents can choose exactly what they want to allow.

speaker: Raja
text: A few essential apps, a recommended set, or the specific ones they want.

speaker: Raja
text: Over time, a child will be ready to add more,

speaker: Raja
text: and parents can remain in control.

speaker: Raja
text: To tell you more, here’s Ann.

speaker: Ann
text: The best way to stay on top of what our kids can do

speaker: Ann
text: is to manage which apps they download.

speaker: Ann
text: On the App Store, every app has an age rating

speaker: Ann
text: that helps us parents decide if it's appropriate for our kids.

speaker: Ann
text: When a child finds an app they want, they need to ask for permission.

speaker: Ann
text: Parents then decide, with helpful context, if it's the right app for their child

speaker: Ann
text: and review these requests right in Messages with Ask to Buy.

speaker: Ann
text: This year we’re expanding what parents can approve

speaker: Ann
text: beyond apps to the web with Ask to Browse.

speaker: Ann
text: Kids need to ask when they want to view a new website.

speaker: Ann
text: Parents can take a closer look before approving.

speaker: Ann
text: This works seamlessly across iPhone, iPad, and Mac with Safari.

speaker: Ann
text: Ask to Browse and Ask to Buy are both on by default for kids under 13.

speaker: Ann
text: Parents can also enable these settings for teens.

speaker: Ann
text: How about who kids can talk to? Parents are in control here, too.

text: and the apps you rely on, and of course

text: In China, Siri AI and the other new Apple Intelligence features

speaker: Alok
text: will not be available while we work through regulatory requirements.

speaker: Alok
text: Next, for developers, there’s a lot to be excited about,

speaker: Alok
text: with new tools and technologies to help you build incredible apps.

speaker: Alok
text: Now you can bring Apple Intelligence right into your apps

speaker: Alok
text: using technologies you already know, like App Intents,

speaker: Alok
text: to help users surface the right information and get things done

speaker: Alok
text: just by asking Siri.

speaker: Alok
text: For example, because the messaging app Line

speaker: Alok
text: indexes its content into Spotlight,

speaker: Alok
text: users can ask Siri

speaker: Alok
text: to help them find information from their conversations in the app.

speaker: Alok
text: Or because the calendar app, Structured, adopts app intents,

speaker: Alok
text: a user can just ask Siri

speaker: Alok
text: to create an event and quickly add it to their timeline, and it's done.

speaker: Alok
text: And so many of you are already building the next generation of intelligent apps -

speaker: Alok
text: powerful, fast, and private using the on-device model

speaker: Alok
text: through the Foundation Models framework.

speaker: Alok
text: You can now use images as input in addition to text.

speaker: Alok
text: Extend the model capabilities with custom skills,

speaker: Alok
text: and even use models running on servers,

speaker: Alok
text: all through the same Swift API.

speaker: Alok
text: For example, many people ask me,

speaker: Alok
text: “How can you possibly do this many presentations

speaker: Alok
text: and always be in some version of a blue shirt?”

speaker: Alok
text: I apply advanced technology.

speaker: Alok
text: With an app like Daydream, I can select a photo from an event

speaker: Alok
text: and the app can use the on-device model to identify every piece of my outfit.

speaker: Alok
text: Then it uses its deep fashion expertise

speaker: Alok
text: to help me find something right within my narrow fashion comfort zone.

speaker: Alok
text: In addition to Apple Foundation models,

speaker: Alok
text: you can now easily bring other models

speaker: Alok
text: to run locally in your app with the full power of Apple silicon,

speaker: Alok
text: using the new Core AI framework available on all our platforms.

speaker: Alok
text: That's apps and intelligence working together.

speaker: Alok
text: And it comes back to what has always made the apps you make so great.

speaker: Alok
text: Rich, native experiences

speaker: Alok
text: now enhanced with intelligence, not replaced by it.

speaker: Alok
text: Our app ecosystem has never been more vibrant.

speaker: Alok
text: More developers, more apps, more innovation than ever before.

speaker: Alok
text: Like with agentic coding, it’s changing how apps get built,

speaker: Alok
text: and Xcode is the best place to build them.

speaker: Alok
text: The coding assistant

speaker: Alok
text: can now localize an entire app and interact with simulated devices.

speaker: Alok
text: And you can extend its capabilities with custom skills.

speaker: Alok
text: You can also choose the model and agent of your choice,

speaker: Alok
text: now including Gemini, and connect to tools like Figma and GitHub.

speaker: Alok
text: And we’re reimagining how you can test apps with the all-new Device Hub

speaker: Alok
text: that brings every device, simulated and real, into one unified interface.

speaker: Alok
text: You can now simulate multi-touch controls,

speaker: Alok
text: like swipe and pinch, change your app’s appearance in one click,

speaker: Alok
text: and even resize it dynamically to iterate like never before.

speaker: Alok
text: You’ll hear much more about our tools,

speaker: Alok
text: languages, and frameworks in the Platforms State of the Union.

speaker: Alok
text: And we have a big week ahead with technical sessions,

speaker: Alok
text: live forums, group labs with Apple engineers, and more.

speaker: Alok
text: It's going to be an amazing WWDC.

speaker: Alok
text: Now, back to Tim.

speaker: Tim
text: Thank you, Craig, and thanks to all of our presenters.

speaker: Tim
text: We're excited to bring a wide range of improvements to each

speaker: Tim
text: of our platforms, making them even more useful and delightful.

speaker: Tim
text: We’re also continuing to provide more features that will help kids

speaker: Tim
text: enjoy their Apple products to the fullest,

speaker: Tim
text: while ensuring their experiences are most appropriate for them.

speaker: Tim
text: And the next generation of Apple Intelligence

speaker: Tim
text: introduces new capabilities, including Siri AI,

speaker: Tim
text: that delivers rich conversations and draws upon your personal context

speaker: Tim
text: to help you get more done.

speaker: Tim
text: This, along with our other new features and capabilities,

speaker: Tim
text: will help users get even more out of our products.

speaker: Tim
text: Our new OS releases will be available as developer betas today,

speaker: Tim
text: with a public beta coming next month.

speaker: Tim
text: And they will be available to users this fall.

speaker: Tim
text: On a personal note,

speaker: Tim
text: some of the greatest highlights of my time as CEO have been events like this.

speaker: Tim
text: Sharing powerful new tools with all of you

speaker: Tim
text: and then seeing what you create with them has been a constant reminder

speaker: Tim
text: that imagination has no limits.

speaker: Tim
text: Over the years you have helped people connect, create,

speaker: Tim
text: learn, and experience the world in extraordinary new ways.

speaker: Tim
text: And with the incredible capabilities we introduced today

speaker: Tim
text: and so many more still to come, I truly believe the best is still ahead.

speaker: Tim
text: At Apple, creating the best products in the world

speaker: Tim
text: to deliver experiences that enrich people’s lives

speaker: Tim
text: has always been our North Star.

speaker: Tim
text: It's been the honor of a lifetime to help advance that mission with teams

speaker: Tim
text: whose creativity, care, and conviction

speaker: Tim
text: continue to make a lasting difference in people’s lives.

speaker: Tim
text: Thank you all for joining us.

speaker: Tim
text: Let’s have a great WWDC!
